old: to weigh
reason: 约 (yuē) does not mean 'to weigh'. All three sentences illustrate 约 as the adverb 'approximately'. Wrong-definition error throughout this row.

old: 这个袋子约三公斤。
reason: Adds 重 so the sentence reads naturally as 'weighs approximately three kilograms' rather than fragmentary.

old: 他约了一下那个东西有多重。
reason: Original uses 约 as a verb 'to weigh', which is incorrect. Replaced with natural adverb usage (approximately).

old: He weighed roughly how heavy that thing was.
new: This road is approximately one kilometer long.

old: 请约一下这些水果有多重。
reason: Original uses 约 as a verb 'to weigh', which is incorrect. Replaced with natural adverb usage.

old: Please weigh approximately how heavy these fruits are.
new: He came back approximately one hour later.

old: 她在等她的约会的人。
reason: 她的约会的人 is unnatural phrasing in Chinese. Replaced with a natural sentence illustrating 约会 as a noun (date).

old: She is waiting for her date.
这个月饼是豆沙馅的。
reason: 豆沙 and 馅 are not in vocab_upto_L4.

old: This mooncake has red bean paste filling.
这里的人越来越多。
reason: Target word 增加 is absent from the original sentence_1. 学生 and 人数 are both in vocab.

old: More and more people are coming here.
这是中国之大。
reason: 这是中国之大 is grammatically incorrect — 这是 cannot predicate 中国之大. Replaced with a grammatical literary construction using 令人感动 (令 is used in 令人, 感动 is in vocab).

old: This is the greatness of China. (之 as literary possessive, equivalent to 的)
new: China's vastness is moving. (之 as literary possessive, equivalent to 的)

old: Afterwards eating, I went for a walk.
new: After eating, I went for a walk.
reason: Unnatural English. 之后 = after, not 'afterwards [verb-ing]'.

old: Afterwards graduating, he went to Beijing.
new: After graduating, he went to Beijing.
reason: Unnatural English translation.

old: Afterwards class, we went to play football.
new: After class, we went to play football.
reason: Unnatural English translation.

old: 老师让我们算这个星球的质量。
new: 科学课上我们学了质量。
reason: 星球 is not in vocab_upto_L4. 科学 is in vocab.

old: The teacher asked us to calculate the mass of this planet.
new: In science class we learned about mass.

old: 窗帘有三重，很厚。
reason: 窗帘 (curtain) is not in vocab_upto_L4. 外套 is in vocab.

old: The curtain has three layers and is very thick.
new: This coat has three layers and is very thick.

old: chóng
reason: 重 meaning 'important' is read zhòng, not chóng (chóng = again/repeat/layers).

old: chóng diǎn
reason: 重点 is pronounced zhòng diǎn. chóng is the wrong reading for this word.

old: 好的周围让人心情好。
reason: 好的周围 used as a nominal head meaning 'environment' is unnatural — 周围 means 'surroundings/vicinity', not 'environment'. Definition is questionable; sentence replaced to use 周围 naturally.

old: A good environment makes people feel happy.
孩子需要一个好的周围。
reason: 一个好的周围 is unnatural — 周围 cannot stand alone as a count noun meaning 'environment'.

old: Children need a good environment.
new: The people around him are all very kind to him.

old: 这里的周围让我很舒服。
reason: Using 周围 as a sentient subject that makes one comfortable is unnatural.

old: The environment here makes me feel very comfortable.
new: The surroundings here are very beautiful.

old: 这个圆圈转得很快。
reason: 圆圈 is not in vocab_upto_L4. 风 and 车 are both in vocab.

old: This circle revolves very quickly.
他把电话转到前台。
reason: 前台 is not in vocab_upto_L4. 经理 is in vocab.

old: He transferred the call to the front desk.
new: She transferred the call to the manager.

old: 我在广州转机去美国。
reason: 美国 is not in vocab_upto_L4. 辛苦 is in vocab.

old: I changed planes in Guangzhou to go to America.
new: She changed planes in Beijing, which was exhausting.

old: to swindle
reason: 赚 (zhuàn) does not mean 'to swindle'. That is a fabricated definition. 赚 means only to earn/profit. This row duplicates row 3011's definition but the sentences must be corrected.

old: 他赚了我很多钱。
reason: Original sentence illustrates swindling (wrong definition). Replaced to illustrate 'to earn money'.

old: He swindled a lot of money from me.
new: He earned quite a lot of money from his part-time job.

old: 小心那个人，他会赚你的。
reason: Original illustrates swindling (wrong definition). Replaced to illustrate 'to earn'.

old: Be careful of that person, he will swindle you.
new: She earns a living by singing every day.

old: 他被人赚走了一大笔钱。
reason: Original illustrates swindling (wrong definition). Replaced to illustrate 'to earn'.

old: He was swindled out of a large sum of money.
new: Only by working hard can you earn more money.

old: 他在门上装了一把锁。
reason: 锁 (lock) is not in vocab_upto_L4. 灯 is in vocab.

old: He installed a lock on the door.
new: He installed a light on the door.

old: 这里不准抽烟。
reason: 抽 is not in vocab_upto_L4. 吸 (line 524 of vocab) is in vocab; 吸烟 = to smoke.

old: Smoking is not permitted here.
王的身边有很多左右。
reason: 王 (king as standalone common noun) is not in vocab_upto_L4.

old: The king had many servants around him.
new: He had two servants by his side.

old: 他的左右都很忠心。
new: 他的左右每天照顾他的生活。
reason: 忠心 is not in vocab_upto_L4.

old: His servants were all very loyal.
new: His servants took care of his daily life.
